dimensions.name: root_cause_correctness
dimensions.note: Root cause is correctly identified and grounded in direct evidence. The per-task manifest at tests/generated/20260526-052559/manifest.json has exactly 6 entries in active_tests (confirmed by reading the file). Both test files test_ac_07_ac07-venv-activate-allowed.py and test_ac_07b_ac07b-forged-arg-blocked.py exist on disk (confirmed by directory listing) with correct AC_UID headers. The root cause — test-writer wrote the manifest after generating AC1-AC6 then added AC7/AC7b files without updating the manifest — is the only coherent explanation and is consistent with the generated_at timestamp 2026-05-26T05:35:00Z. Evidence type is correctly marked 'inferred' (no git history for untracked generated artifacts). Diagnosis is complete.

dimensions.name: scope_correctness
dimensions.note: Scope is correctly bounded. The per-task manifest (tests/generated/20260526-052559/manifest.json) requires a write to append two entries. The global manifest (tests/generated/manifest.json) already contains the task entry with manifest_path='tests/generated/20260526-052559/manifest.json' (confirmed by parsing the file). The BA correctly classifies the global manifest as read-verify only. No test file content changes, no schema_version bumps, no global manifest structural changes — all correctly excluded. The Won't Have list is appropriate and does not exclude anything the user requirement requires.

dimensions.name: ac_completeness
dimensions.note: Four ACs cover the complete requirement. AC1 verifies the count postcondition (len==8). AC2 verifies all fields of the ac-07 entry including hook_check.expected_exit=0. AC3 verifies all fields of the ac-07b entry including hook_check.expected_exit=2 and hook_check.expected_stderr_token='bulk-commit-auth-flag-write'. AC4 verifies global manifest read state. All ACs use json_parse method which is mechanically executable. The hook_check field assertions in AC2/AC3 do not assert the method string value, which is appropriate since 'pytest or bash subprocess' is a documentation field, not a constraint. The minimal hook_check schema prescribed ({method, expected_exit} for ac-07 and {method, expected_exit, expected_stderr_token} for ac-07b) correctly matches the ac-03 and ac-02 canonical forms in the existing manifest, with the extra command/description fields dropped per Codex review. This is internally consistent.

dimensions.name: artifact_consistency
dimensions.note: All three artifacts are mutually consistent. The new_entries block in context JSON exactly matches what the ticket Technical Hints prescribe and what the ACs assert. The ac_uid values ('ac07-venv-activate-allowed', 'ac07b-forged-arg-blocked') match both the test file AC_UID headers and the new_entries. The file paths are identical across ticket, context, and ACs. The hook_check schema in context JSON new_entries matches the AC check assertions. The requirements_decomposition R1/R2 in the context JSON maps cleanly to the ticket's Must Have / Won't Have. The ticket's AC4 (global manifest verification) corresponds to R2 and the scope classification 'read-verify only'. No contradictions found between any of the three artifacts.

dimensions.name: risk_coverage
dimensions.note: Risk is correctly assessed as low. This is a manifest JSON append with no hook logic, no test logic, and no infrastructure change. The only regression risk is accidentally corrupting the existing 6 entries or producing invalid JSON — both are mitigated by the AC1 count assertion and the field-level assertions in AC2/AC3. One schema observation: ac-01 in the existing manifest has a 'command' field in hook_check that other entries (ac-02, ac-03) do not have. BA explicitly instructs dev to use the minimal form (no command/description) for the two new entries. This is correct — there is no requirement that all entries be structurally identical, and the minimal form is valid. The Codex adversarial review was invoked and applied (dropped non-canonical fields). test_writer_expected=false is correct for this SMALL manifest-only fix. blast_radius_map_path=null is correct given no code-path changes.

non_blocking_observations.observation: AC2 and AC3 do not assert the hook_check.method field value. This is intentional and correct — the method string is a human-readable documentation label, not a constraint that QA tooling enforces. No change needed.

The existing ac-01 manifest entry retains a 'command' field in hook_check that the two new entries will not have. BA's decision to use the minimal schema for new entries (matching ac-02/ac-03 patterns) is well-justified by Codex review and creates no inconsistency — the manifest schema does not require uniform hook_check shape across entries.

root_cause_analysis.evidence_type is 'inferred' rather than 'direct'. This is the correct classification: the manifest is an untracked generated artifact with no git history. The inferred evidence is of high quality (direct file read + directory listing) and the diagnosis confidence is appropriate.
